qa_pairs.question: What happened to cloud instances in the Juno Cloud non-High Availability zone?
qa_pairs.answer: Cloud instances in the Juno Cloud non-High Availability (non-HA) zone were shut down.

qa_pairs.question: How were jobs scheduled to finish after 9:00 a.m. on June 6 affected?
qa_pairs.answer: Jobs scheduled to finish after 9:00 a.m. on June 6 remained queued until the clusters were back online.

qa_pairs.question: Did Béluga Cloud instances experience any issues during the maintenance?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, cloud instances on Béluga Cloud experienced short access outages.

qa_pairs.question: Were Juno Cloud instances in the High Availability zone affected by the work?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, cloud instances in the Juno Cloud High Availability (HA) zone experienced short access outages.

qa_pairs.question: Was storage on Béluga and Narval accessible during the maintenance period?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, storage systems on Béluga and Narval remained accessible via Globus and login nodes, but they did experience intermittent disruptions.

qa_pairs.question: What caused the disruptions to cloud instances and storage systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Brief interruptions for network and storage maintenance caused the disruptions to cloud instances and storage systems.
